tips.tip: include common resources such as pricing page embed, customer case studies, and product playbooks in Buyer Hub
tips.reasoning: provide relevant resources in one place so prospects don't need to leave for complete evaluation
tips.outcome: improve user experience and reduce friction

tips.tip: organize Buyer Hubs by audience roles as well rather than just product features
tips.reasoning: Role-based organization increases relevance compared to generic feature categorization

tips.tip: title your demo chapters based on use cases or outcomes, not just features
tips.reasoning: capability or use-focused naming provide a more compelling demo experience than technical feature descriptions
tips.outcome: improve user understanding and click-through rates

tips.tip: use brand colors in demo elements such as button, spotlight, beacons and text modals
tips.reasoning: Consistent brand colors throughout demo guides create professional cohesion and reinforces visual identity during the evaluation experience

tips.tip: end demos with a celebratory step and clear next steps
tips.reasoning: Achievement language triggers satisfaction and motivates prospects to take action
tips.outcome: improve completion rates and user satisfaction

tips.tip: include step counters within your guides for ease of navigation
tips.reasoning: Visible step counters help users understand their position and time remaining
tips.outcome: improve completion rates and user orientation

tips.tip: enable 'setup navigation' for users to track demo progress
tips.reasoning: Progress indicators and navigation controls help users understand their position in the demo and provide confidence about time investment remaining.

tips.tip: avoid autoplay for marketing demos on websites
tips.reasoning: user-controlled pacing improves active engagement and helps track steps your prospects spend more time on to guage interest points within the demos
tips.outcome: improve completion rates and user orientation

tips.tip: deploy 2-in-1 sandbox + guided demos
tips.reasoning: wondering if you should share a guided demo or sandbox environment? use Storylane's "exit guide" option on HTML demos to support both in a single demo — better flexibilty for users

tips.tip: use text triggers to personalize environments and simulate hands on product experience
tips.reasoning: Having users input text or data creates active participation and gives prospects a realistic feel of actually using the product.

tips.tip: use emojis in demo copy for visual appeal and engagement
tips.reasoning: Strategic emoji use for tools or celebration icons breaks up text-heavy content and adds personality to otherwise sterile product demos.
tips.outcome: improve visual appeal and user engagement

tips.tip: highlight your latest releases with "New!" tags in guide copy
tips.reasoning: highlighting "New!" with distinct formatting in your guide modals is a low-hanging way to excite prospects and convey your latest features

tips.tip: endorse your demo with case studies and social proof
tips.reasoning: pepper in case studies, testimonials, and ROI data in your guides to help prospects validate your product's value
tips.outcome: improve demo completion, improve conversion rates, accelerate buy-in

tips.tip: use a mascot or theme to give your demos character
tips.reasoning: Consistent mascots or themes make demos memorable and differentiate your experience from generic product walkthroughs.
tips.outcome: improve demo completion and brand differentiation

tips.tip: link to help documentation for complex features
tips.reasoning: Optional deep-dive links provide context without overwhelming the main demo flow
tips.outcome: improve user support and reduce confusion

tips.tip: format in-guide copy to highlight value props, selling points, and outcomes
tips.reasoning: Bold text and formatted copy makes demo steps more scannable and helps prospects quickly identify key information and benefits.

tips.tip: highlight your upcoming features with "Coming Soon!" tags in guide copy
tips.reasoning: Showing roadmap features with "Coming Soon" tags addresses prospect objections about missing functionality during the evaluation process.

tips.tip: get creative with PII masking by using a theme
tips.reasoning: Themed data (like pirate names, adventure scenarios) makes demos more engaging while protecting real customer information
tips.outcome: improve engagement while maintaining data privacy

tips.tip: include expected results within demo steps
tips.reasoning: Expected results and outcomes grab attention during demo and provide concrete value validation instead of just showing features.

tips.tip: deploy persistent convert CTA buttons for easy access at any point during the demo
tips.reasoning: Floating "book demo" buttons allow conversion at peak interest without relying on demo completion.
tips.outcome: improve conversion rates and user flexibility

tips.tip: when using multiple CTAs, have a "small" ask and a "big" ask
tips.reasoning: our demo experiments reveal that when using multiple CTAs on the same step, a combination of a "small ask" (Eg: check out pricing) and a "big ask" (Eg: book a demo) performs significantly better than two big asks (Eg: "Start free" and "Book a demo"

tips.tip: communicate value/fomo in gated demo form copy
tips.reasoning: If you choose to gate your demo, use copy along the lines of "we're preparing a demo for you..." to convey a sense of personalization, not friction. This ensures that your form feel like a part of the experience, not a barrier to it.
tips.outcome: improve lead generation, improve conversion rates

tips.tip: soft gate your demo at a cliffhanger
tips.reasoning: tease out the first few steps of your demo and drop a gate an "aha moment" or "cliffhanger" to maximize engagement AND lead generation
tips.outcome: improve lead generation, improve conversion rates

tips.tip: specify the pricing tier in the demo step, where the showcased feature can be found
tips.reasoning: Transparent pricing during feature demonstration prevents sticker shock and builds trust.
tips.outcome: reduce sales cycle friction and improve lead quality

tips.tip: deploy multiple conversion CTAs for PLG + SLG companies
tips.reasoning: by default, Storylane supports one convert CTA button but you can additional CTAs with custom code, giving users the choice to pick between sign-ups, demo calls, and more

tips.tip: auto-populate form fields using visitor data enrichment
tips.reasoning: Reduce form friction by pre-filling known company and contact information
tips.outcome: improve conversion rates and reduce abandonment

tips.tip: use voiceovers strategically to break the monotony of text
tips.reasoning: use voiceovers strategically to improve accessibility and retain attention through the length of the demo

tips.tip: screen recording for workflow sequences and transitions
tips.reasoning: screen recording show process flow better than static screenshots for complex workflows

tips.tip: add a face to your demo with presetner recordings or AI generated video avatars for a human touch
tips.reasoning: Human faces create trust and connection compared to faceless voiceovers or impersonal experiences

tips.tip: use a leadgen form + dynamic tokens to personalise your demos on the spot
tips.reasoning: If you're gating your demos, consider using text tokens to personalize the demo and demo guides based on collected lead gen information to add a personal touch to the demo experience

tips.tip: place your demos on high-intent pages (eg: pricing)
tips.reasoning: your pricing page is a great example of a high-traffic, high-intent touchpoint where prospects pause and evaluate — prime real estate to educate buyers with a demo

tips.tip: use interactive demos as exit-intent popups to re-engage abandoning visitors
tips.reasoning: Exit-intent demos capture visitors about to leave by offering immediate product value instead of traditional email signup forms.
tips.outcome: improve visitor retention and conversion rates

tips.tip: in case your product uses desktop and mobile applications, incorporate screens from both devices for a complete demo experience
tips.reasoning: if your product supports both desktop and mobile apps, combine screens from both int oa single demo for a complete product experience

tips.tip: include customer testimonials within guided demo steps
tips.reasoning: Mid-demo social proof builds credibility during evaluation vs end-loaded testimonials

tips.tip: keep your guide copy short but expand your demo narrative through presenter videos and voiceovers
tips.reasoning: we recommend limiting the copy on your guide widgets to under 15 words, but make sure to expand on key steps with videos and voiceovers to get the message across

tips.tip: use consistent themes and visual narratives
tips.reasoning: thematic consistency makes demos memorable compared to generic product presentations

tips.tip: avoid capturing demos on large screens or external monitors for consistent UX across devices
tips.reasoning: Large monitors make the product interface appear. Standard laptops capture screens and ensure UI elements remain readable across different devices.

tips.tip: hook prospects with relevant pain points in your welcome screen
tips.reasoning: surfacing relevant pain points (especially in a humourous way) in your welcome screen is a great hook to your target audiences in — and instant way to convey: "hey, we get it!"

tips.tip: encourage mobile friendly demo experiences. lead with rotate screen or "add cue's to improve mobile experience"
tips.reasoning: if your demos recieve considerable mobile traffic, share a quick note about rotating the device (horizontally) for a better experience

tips.tip: hook prosepcts with relavant data points in your welcome screen
tips.reasoning: data points on your product's results/ROI is a compelling way to reel users into your demo

tips.tip: walk users through how your demo environment works with an intro video
tips.reasoning: for users that may not be familiar with guided and sandbox demos, a quick explainer video atop your demo can be extremely helpful to provide context and drive engagement

tips.tip: split your demo into distinct flows using multiple CTAs
tips.reasoning: if you have a couple of key productlines, use cases, or personas, use the primary and secondary CTA in your opening guide to let users pick what they'd like to explore — an alternative to full fledged chapters!

tips.tip: hook prospects with an engaging, humorous welcome screen
tips.reasoning: a humorous welcome screen is a great scroll-stopper, especially if the bit is relevant to your target audience. Consider using a meme, a video, or even a celebrity cameo!

tips.tip: hook prospects with a pointed, relevant question + CTA
tips.reasoning: opening with targeted questions immediately engages prospects and creates curiosity that motivates them to continue through the demo experience.
tips.outcome: improve initial engagement and click-through rates

tips.tip: include time commitment in demo CTAs to set clear expectations
tips.reasoning: Adding time estimates like "Storylane in 5 minutes" helps prospects commit appropriate time and reduces abandonment from unknown time investment
tips.outcome: improve completion rates and user commitment

tips.tip: keep your demos short
tips.reasoning: We recommend limited your demos to between 8-12 steps. If you need to more, consider splitting your demo into chapters.

tips.tip: craft a story, not a "how to"
tips.reasoning: To create engaging demos, avoid dry "how to" style product walkthrough content. Instead, create compelling narratives that address relevant pain points and use cases.

suggestions.suggestion: This demo has fewer than 6 steps. Add a couple of more steps to tell a complete story.
With 15+ steps, this demo is getting a little long. Try cutting down to 12 steps to keep viewers from losing steam.
This demo has more than 20 steps. Consider breaking longer demos it into chapters or Buyer Hubs.
Your guides seem to be running long. Aim for under 200 characters so viewers actually read through them.
This demo has no call to action. Consider adding a convert CTA button ("Book a demo", "Start for free", "View pricing") to encourage next steps.
No lead form yet! Add one (perhaps at a cliffhanger) to capture leads and turn interest into conversions.
Consider adding videos and voiceover to guide viewers through — it's way more engaging than clicking solo.
Some colors look off-brand. Clean them up to keep everything looking sharp and professional.
Add navigation controls so viewers can jump around and explore at their own pace.
Track and zoom helps viewers focus on what matters. Add it to guide their eyes where you want them.
Screen sizes are jumping around. Standardize dimensions for a cleaner, more polished experience.
Start strong! Add a welcome screen with a video, GIF, or image to set the stage for what's coming.
